berm: noun. A narrow ledge or shelf, as along the top or bottom of a slope. (Can we verify this sense?)
brr: interjection. Used to express being cold, to show shivering.
ember: noun. A piece of coal or wood glowing by heat; a hot coal.
emmer: noun. Any of species Triticum dicoccum, one of a group of hulled wheats that are important food grains.
mem: noun. The thirteenth letter of many Semitic alphabets/abjads (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Syriac, Arabic and others).
member: noun. One who belongs to a group.
mere: adjective. Just, only; no more than, pure and simple, neither more nor better than might be expected.
merer: adjective. comparative form of mere: more mere
reb: noun. Clipping of rebel: a Confederate, especially a Confederate soldier.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).

remember: verb. To recall from one's memory; to have an image in one's memory.
beme: noun. A mediaeval trumpet, usually used in hunting or warfare.
ber: noun. A fruit-bearing tree (Ziziphus mauritiana); Indian jujube.
bere: noun. Barley, especially four-rowed barley or six-rowed barley.
bmr: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Muinane.
erme: verb. To grieve; to feel sad.
